_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of how other actors may affect operations
reference_document_element_description: Ability to navigate ethical trade-offs in AI deployment where competing values such as fairness versus accuracy, transparency versus privacy, or speed versus safety require principled resolution.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI AG domain. AG-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-S-001 (RM, cosine: 0.436), AG-K-002 (AG, cosine: 0.425).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 1.7/10 (raw 0.169). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [actors, affect, knowledge, operations] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, accuracy, ai, competing, deployment, ethical] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.849 (P=0.864, R=0.834). P exceeds R by 0.031 — the DCWF element's 8 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 26 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Knowledge of how other actors may affect operations" and "Ability to navigate ethical trade-offs in AI deployment where competing values s" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.441 > STS 0.169 (gap +0.273). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [actors, affect, knowledge, operations]. WIDAI terms: [ability, accuracy, ai, competing, deployment, ethical].
